event: never left the bed or nursing chair
could not open her mouth over 20 degrees
stenosis of the internal axis carotis
fistula between the esophagus and tracheal membrane
referred to the University of Erlangen
examined by several chiefs and consultants
deemed too unstable for open surgery
put on veno-venous extracorporeal membrane oxygenation
big fistula of the tracheal membrane
tracheal cannula ended shortly beneath the lower limit of the mediastinal fistula
plan to close the fistula with a pedicled omentum majus replacement
surgical plastic needed an abutment and a secured continuous airway replacement
vv-ECMO began to be partly ineffective
high volume input of physiological saline
oral approach would only allow a small flexible bronchoscope
approach for this upper part of the trachea had to be performed through the percutaneous tracheostoma
trying different Dumon and one-hybrid self expandable metalic y-stent
plan was to changeover to a more floppy Freitag stent
whole procedure was accompanied by a mandatory additional ventilation
nasal jet catheter or a special double-lumen endotracheal tube exchange catheter
ventilation line was introduced either orally or through the tracheostoma
placed distally below the main carina
successful retrograde stenting was performed in four steps
manually compressed “y” of the FS was successfully pushed downward on the main carina
frontal surface of the stent was cut with at least 1 cm opening in the longitudinal axis
stent surface was reduced ~40% in the sagittal axis
new stoma for a regular tracheal cannula was created
lower new edge of this stoma was fixed subcutaneously
jagwire was introduced through the mouth into the trachea
jagwire was running out of the new FS stoma
leaving the dorsal membrane of the cut FS behind
soft-tip stiff DLET was introduced for more stability
dragged out of the new stoma of the cut FS
distal jagwire was introduced into the oral orifice of the FS
oral part of the FS was still outside the body
bending the FS outward at the level of percutaneous stoma
pulling the jagwire at both ends
FS flipped with its upper part over the soft-tip stiff DLET into the upper third of the trachea
whole fistula was bridged by the FS up to the level of the vocal cords
upper edge of the new FS stoma was fixed subcutaneously
regular tracheal cannula was introduced for ventilation
lungs were not aerated at that point of time
could communicate with her family by writing and her eyes
infections continued to be very severe
spontaneous work of breathing never exceeded a tidal ventilation of 170 mL per breath
reduction of intravenous saline injection was limited
patient along with her family decided actively to reduce the vv-ECMO support
